claims.claim: Synergy Research global data center capacity split: hyperscale 48%, non-hyperscale colocation 20%, enterprise on-premise 32%, as of end-2025.
claims.sourceTitle: Hyperscale Operators to Account for 67% of all Data Center Capacity by 2031 | Synergy Research Group
claims.excerpt: they now account for 48% of the worldwide capacity of all data centers... With non-hyperscale colocation capacity accounting for another 20% of total capacity, that leaves enterprise on-premise data centers with just 32% of the total.

claims.claim: CBRE: hyperscaler self-build capacity across Europe expected to reach 4.2 gigawatts in 2026, representing 24% year-on-year growth vs 2025.
claims.sourceTitle: New hyperscaler self-build capacity growth to outpace colocation supply growth in Europe | News | Institutional Real Estate, Inc.
claims.excerpt: the latest research shows hyperscaler self-build capacity across Europe is expected to reach 4.2 gigawatts this year, representing 24 percent year-on-year growth compared to 2025.

claims.claim: CBRE: as of Q4 2025, approximately 60% of Europe's operational hyperscaler self-build capacity is located in Ireland, the Netherlands, Sweden and Belgium — not Central European CE-7 countries.
claims.sourceTitle: New hyperscaler self-build capacity growth to outpace colocation supply growth in Europe | News | Institutional Real Estate, Inc.
claims.excerpt: As of fourth quarter 2025, approximately 60 percent of Europe's operational hyperscaler self-build capacity is located in Ireland, the Netherlands, Sweden and Belgium.

claims.claim: CBRE: top 15 European colocation data center markets expected to grow 19% (implied YoY, compared against hyperscaler self-build growth of 24%).
claims.sourceTitle: New hyperscaler self-build capacity growth to outpace colocation supply growth in Europe | News | Institutional Real Estate, Inc.
claims.excerpt: By comparison, the top 15 European colocation data center markets are expected to grow 19 per[cent]

claims.claim: CBRE 2025 outlook: data centre supply across Europe's five largest markets expected to grow by a record 20% year-on-year in 2025.
claims.excerpt: data centre supply across Europe's five largest markets is expected to grow by a record 20% year-on-year in 2025.

claims.claim: CBRE: London and Frankfurt expected to account for 2.5GW of capacity, approximately half of total European data centre supply.
claims.excerpt: London and Frankfurt are expected to account for 2.5GW of capacity, or approximately half of the total data centre supply in Europe

claims.claim: Structure Research: Frankfurt market worth just over €2b in 2024, projected to grow at a 5-year CAGR of 19.4%; hyperscale colocation growing at a five-year CAGR of 26.1%, retail colocation at 10.3%.
claims.sourceTitle: Frankfurt DCI Report 2024: Data Centre Colocation, Hyperscale Cloud & Interconnection - Structure Research
claims.excerpt: This is a market that will be worth just over €2b in 2024 and is projected to grow at a 5-year CAGR of 19.4%. Hyperscale colocation is growing at a five-year CAGR of 26.1% and retail colocation is moving along at a 10.3% clip.

claims.claim: Structure Research: Frankfurt built-out capacity was nearly 650MW in 2022, projected to climb to over 900MW by end of 2024.
claims.sourceTitle: Frankfurt DCI Report 2024: Data Centre Colocation, Hyperscale Cloud & Interconnection - Structure Research
claims.excerpt: The current built-out capacity of the market was nearly 650MW in 2022 and is projected to climb to over 900MW by the end of 2024.

claims.claim: Structure Research: Frankfurt built-out capacity will exceed 1GW by end of 2025 and tip over 2GW in 2029; 2.7GW of credible future capacity tracked at various stages.
claims.sourceTitle: Frankfurt DCAI Report 2025: Data Centre Colocation, Hyperscale Cloud, AI & Interconnection - Structure Research
claims.excerpt: Frankfurt built-out capacity will exceed 1GW by the end of 2025 and tip over 2GW in 2029. Structure Research is tracking 2.7GW of credible future capacity at various stages of construction/planning.

claims.claim: Mordor Intelligence: Poland hyperscale data center market size was USD 385.87 million in 2025, forecast to reach USD 2,374.49 million in 2031 at 35.37% CAGR; installed IT load grows from 564.11 MW to 1,086.70 MW at an 11.55% CAGR.
claims.sourceTitle: Poland Hyperscale Data Center Market Growth Report 2031
claims.excerpt: The Poland hyperscale data center market size stood at USD 385.87 million in 2025 and is forecast to climb to USD 2,374.49 million in 2031, advancing at a striking 35.37% CAGR over the period while installed IT load grows from 564.11 MW to 1,086.70 MW at an 11.55% CAGR.

claims.claim: Arizton report covers 341 data center facilities (290 existing, 51 upcoming, 150 operators) across 4 Central & Eastern Europe countries (Austria, Czech Republic, Poland, and one more), but does not present a hyperscale-vs-colocation capacity split.
claims.sourceTitle: Central & Eastern Europe Data Centers: Existing & Upcoming Data Centers
claims.excerpt: Discover Insights on 341 Data Center Facilities Across 4 Key Countries in Central & Eastern Europe... 290 Existing Data Centers 51 Upcoming Data Centers 150 Data Center Operators

claims.claim: EUDCA 'State of European Data Centres 2026' report contains dedicated sections 'Colocation in Europe' and 'Hyperscale-Owned Data Centres', but no numeric split was surfaced in the retrieved text.
claims.excerpt:
Overview of the Data Centre Landscape 8 
Colocation in Europe 11 
Hyperscale-Owned Data Centres 24

tensions.text: CBRE finds ~60% of Europe's operational hyperscaler self-build capacity sits in Ireland, Netherlands, Sweden and Belgium — not in CE-7 markets — which undercuts the seg.hyperscale node's method of rescaling Synergy's *global* 44-48% hyperscale capacity share directly onto Central Europe via a flat 0.91 carve-out factor; if hyperscale self-build is disproportionately concentrated in Western/Northern Europe, CE's true hyperscale-owned share could be materially lower than the derived 0.44.

Structure Research's Frankfurt-specific data shows hyperscale colocation growing at 26.1% CAGR vs retail colocation at 10.3% CAGR — a regionally-grounded growth differential that could be used to sanity-check or refine the segment split, but it describes Frankfurt only, not CE-7, and is not reconcilable with the global Synergy-derived split used in seg.hyperscale and seg.colocation.

absences: No Europe- or CE-7-scoped capacity split (hyperscale-owned vs colocation share of operational MW) was found from CBRE, Structure Research, or Synergy — despite CE-7 country names (Poland, Czechia, Austria, Hungary) appearing explicitly in the query intent, none of the retrieved sources published a regional ownership-type percentage breakdown.

Structure Research's Frankfurt DCAI/DCI reports (2024, 2025) are paywalled ($8,500) and their public previews describe market value/CAGR by segment type but do not disclose a hyperscale-vs-colocation MW percentage split.
Arizton's CEE portfolio report catalogs facility counts and operators but its public preview does not break capacity down by ownership type (hyperscale self-build vs colocation).

EUDCA's 'State of European Data Centres 2026' has a dedicated 'Hyperscale-Owned Data Centres' chapter (p.24) that plausibly contains the needed split, but its content was not retrieved/quantified in these search results.

No Poland-, Czechia-, Austria-, or Hungary-specific hyperscale-owned-vs-colocation percentage figures were found; only Poland's aggregate hyperscale market value/MW growth (Mordor) surfaced, with no colocation comparator or ownership share stated.
